prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde were computed using **DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (tier standard, PySCF) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The calculation involved three SCF runs: the neutral molecule (N), the cation (N−1, charge +1, multiplicity 2), and the anion (N+1, charge −1, multiplicity 2). All states converged successfully. Fukui descriptors (f⁺ for electrophilicity, f⁻ for nucleophilicity) and the dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) were computed via atomic charge differences. The most electrophilic site is atom 3 (aromatic C, ortho-CH) with f⁺ = 0.3994; the most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (carbonyl O) with f⁻ = 0.2384. Charge conservation checks (∑f⁺ = 1.0000, ∑f⁻ = 1.0000) passed, confirming reliable convergence. A bar-chart visualization was generated. The structure contained 14 atoms (benzaldehyde: C₇H₆O).</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.39938968
